summary: Task 3 implements exactly the one-token argv change ('*:E' -> '*:W' at LogcatReader.kt:37) and the matching pin-test assertion update at LogcatReaderTest.kt:102. Acceptance criteria AC-L1 (filter now includes WARN) and AC-L2 (placeholder/timeout/drain semantics untouched) are preserved; LogExportManager.kt is correctly left alone per the task constraint. The only latent issue surfaced by the change is a stale KDoc phrase on LogcatReader.kt line 12 ('at ERROR level') which now mis-describes runtime behavior — flagged as a nit because the task explicitly forbade further edits to LogcatReader.kt, so the deviation is intentional and best deferred to a follow-up.

findings.issue: KDoc class header still reads 'Reads the current process's own logcat at ERROR level...' but the filter is now '*:W' (WARN and above). Stale documentation will mislead future readers into thinking only ERROR is captured, contradicting AC-L1 and the task commit message.
findings.fix: Defer to a follow-up change (Task 3 explicitly bans further edits to LogcatReader.kt beyond argv[5]). When unblocked, update the KDoc summary to 'at WARN level and above' (or equivalent) to keep doc and code aligned. Severity is nit because (a) the doc is non-load-bearing — runtime behavior is governed solely by argv[5], (b) the task constraint takes precedence over the cleanup, and (c) the next phase-level doc audit will catch it cheaply.
